AR1.justification: Checklist and Go/No-go landed in the runbook's specified file; daybook entry captured operational log, and the agent explicitly justified not appending to decisions.md or observations.md because findings were operational rather than future-constraining commitments.

AR2.justification: Step 1 produced the named checklist artifact at the runbook's canonical path, and step 2's Go/No-go was written into that same file referencing checklist items by name, so the synthesis step is bound to and reads from the prior artifact.

AR3.justification: All references (runbooks/release-readiness-check/, tmp/release-readiness/v2.4/checklist.md, tmp/release-2.4/notes.md, daybook/2026-05-17.md) use relative workspace paths with ISO date anchors and resolve to actual or correctly-flagged-absent locations.

AR4.justification: Checklist is written to a release-scoped subpath (v2.4/), daybook uses an ISO-dated filename, and no in-place mutation of decisions/observations occurred, so a re-run for a different release or session would not conflict.
